preamble: The transformation from an alveolar flap to a voiceless glottal fricative is a form of debuccalization, where the oral articulation of the rhotic is lost, leaving only a glottal breath.

examples.note: Coda rhotics are frequently debuccalized in dialects like Puerto Rican or Cuban (e.g., 'porque' > 'pohke').
The historical alveolar trill/flap has debuccalized to [h] or [x] in many positions.

sources: Lipski, J. M. (1994). Latin American Spanish.
Coutinho, I. L. (1976). Pontos de gramática histórica.
